changes.motivation: The `pf buildkit suspend` command was crashing inside its Kubernetes pod because the CLI's startup routine calls `getDevshellConfig` → `getRoot` → `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`, which requires a git repository that doesn't exist in-container. The sibling `resume` command already had `requiresDevshell = false` set correctly, but `suspend` was missing it.
changes.summary: Added `static override requiresDevshell = false` to `BuildkitScaleDownCommand` in `src/commands/buildkit/suspend/command.ts`, removed the `getAllRegions` import and the `--context` option validation block that depended on it (since `getAllRegions` also requires devshell config), and updated a stale `@remarks` comment in `execute()`.

problems: The `requiresDevshell` flag is the single mechanism preventing the CLI startup from running `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`, but this is not documented on the base class or in command authoring guides. A developer adding a new command that runs in a container has no signal that omitting this flag will cause a git lookup failure at runtime.

The `suspend` and `resume` commands are symmetric operations deployed in the same pod, but only `resume` had `requiresDevshell = false`. The asymmetry was silent — no tests or CI checks caught that `suspend` would fail in-cluster.

`getAllRegions` performs devshell-dependent work, but its name gives no indication of this constraint. A developer using it in a `requiresDevshell = false` command would not realise the dependency until a runtime failure.
